title: FormPositionFromInside
text: FormPositionFromInside Determines how the client area of a primary resizable form is calculated—from the inside or the outside of the upper left corner of the window. In some situations, this can affect whether a horizontal scroll bar is displayed on the bottom of the window. Syntax FormPositionFromInside { = } On | Off Arguments On —calculate the client area from the upper left corner is on the inside of the sizeable form. Off —calculate the client area from the upper left corner on the outside of the window. This is the default. Defaults Default value Off Section [upi] Description By default, a resizable primary form, with the horizontal position set to 1 is displayed without a horizontal scrollbar. Prior to 9.6.02, such a scrollbar was displayed on Windows Vista and Windows 7. Use this switch only if you want to have the old behavior. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
